behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is at real git rename detection plus CLI/report wiring, not a single pure function. MOCK-HIDE applies: stubbing parsed statuses can miss real cases where git emits rename records differently (similarity thresholds/status formats). REAL-INFRA applies because validating this reliably needs a real repo state and CLI execution path. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency (disqualifies Agentic) and no multi-agent sequence (disqualifies Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that change window, and assert report shows 1 rename with 0 corresponding add/delete for that file. Execute through the real CLI entrypoint and stdout parsing.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Crash risk usually appears when real diff output/bytes flow through subprocess and decoding/parsing layers. In-process fakes often hide encoding and binary-marker edge cases (MOCK-HIDE). REAL-INFRA applies due binary content handling in actual repo/diff behavior and CLI execution. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not depend on model judgment (disqualifies Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, add/modify at least one binary file (e.g., image/blob), run the CLI, assert zero crash (exit code 0) and binary-including counts are present/accurate in report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real initial-repo/git command behavior (unborn HEAD, exit codes/messages) flowing through CLI error handling. A mock can force a happy deterministic response and miss true no-commit edge behavior (MOCK-HIDE + REAL-INFRA). Rejected higher levels: no LLM dependency and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a brand-new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert successful execution and an empty structured report (not an exception/error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness across history extraction, categorization, aggregation, and report emission. Real repository history is needed to catch interaction bugs across modules and git semantics; pure unit tests of counters are insufficient. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about git/history accounting only, with no AI/ML decision quality (disqualifies Agentic) and no chained agentic stages (disqualifies Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a controlled sequence of commits containing add/delete/rename/modify operations, run the CLI over that history scope, and assert each category count equals independently computed ground truth from the repo.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is at the index vs working-tree boundary, which is real git state behavior plus CLI option/path handling. In-process stubs can miss true staged/unstaged semantics and diff-source differences (MOCK-HIDE, REAL-INFRA). Rejected higher levels: no model output dependency (disqualifies Agentic) and no multi-agent sequence (disqualifies Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once staged and once unstaged, run the CLI for each mode, and assert the structured stdout reports are identical.
